title: myriophyllum spicatum subsp. exalbescens
intro: Myriophyllum spicatum subsp. exalbescens, also known as the spiny water-fern, is a subspecies of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Haloragaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat various health conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and skin infections, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Myriophyllum spicatum subsp. exalbescens can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
